Title: GmPLP1 negatively regulates soybean resistance to high light stress by modulating photosynthetic capacity and reactive oxygen species accumulation in a blue lightdependent manner

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: antioxidant; reactive oxygen species scavenging; protection against high light stress
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Negatively regulates ascorbic acid accumulation and high-light tolerance; promotes ROS accumulation and light damage under high light; reduces photosynthetic capacity and yield-related traits when overexpressed.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNAi and overexpression in soybean; chlorophyll fluorescence (Fv/Fm); ROS staining and H2O2 measurement; total AsA measurement; protein-protein interaction assays (Y2H, pull-down, BiFC, LCA); transient leaf expression; GGP enzyme activity assay
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: mRNA and protein abundance decrease under high white light; protein strongly responds to blue light intensity, induced at low blue light but reduced under high blue light
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: mRNA and protein abundance decrease under high white light; protein strongly responds to blue light intensity, induced at low blue light but reduced under high blue light
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: high white light (1800 μmol m−2 s−1); low white light (50 μmol m−2 s−1); low blue light (5 μmol m−2 s−1); high blue light (500 μmol m−2 s−1); low red light (5 μmol m−2 s−1); high red light (600 μmol m−2 s−1)
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: self promoter proGmPLP1 for tagged overexpression/detection; constitutive transient overexpression from pCAMBIA3300-GFP constructs; RNAi via pFGC5941
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Overexpression causes weaker growth, smaller leaf area, lower photosynthetic capacity, more ROS, stronger photodamage, and reduced yield-related performance; RNAi improves growth and yield.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Acts as a blue-light-sensing negative regulator of GGP activity via direct protein interaction rather than as a metabolic enzyme or transcription factor. LOV-domain photoactive residues C285 and R286 are important for blue-light inhibition of interaction with GmVTC2b. Transcriptome suggests additional effects on oxidative-stress-related genes and GmPHOT1 under high light.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GmPLP1 abundance is suppressed by high light, especially high blue light. 2. GmPLP1 negatively regulates high-light tolerance by lowering photosynthetic capacity and increasing ROS accumulation. 3. GmPLP1 physically interacts with GmVTC2 proteins, especially characterized with GmVTC2b, through the LOV domain. 4. Blue light weakens GmPLP1-GmVTC2b interaction. 5. GmPLP1 inhibits GGP enzyme activity, reducing ascorbic acid biosynthesis under high light.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Knockdown/editing of GmPLP1 is a promising strategy to increase high-light tolerance, photosynthetic performance, and yield in soybean; should be considered for environments with strong or fluctuating light.

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: antioxidant; reactive oxygen species scavenging; protection against high light stress
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes ascorbic acid accumulation, reduces ROS accumulation, and improves tolerance to high light stress.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Stable overexpression in soybean; AsA quantification; chlorophyll fluorescence (Fv/Fm); ROS staining and H2O2 measurement; transient co-expression with GmPLP1 perturbation; protein interaction assays
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Protein low in darkness and low white light, accumulates under high white light; induced under high blue and high red light
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Protein low in darkness and low white light, accumulates under high white light; induced under high blue and high red light
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: high white light; low white light; blue light intensity; red light intensity
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: self promoter proGmVTC2b for tagged overexpression/detection; constitutive transient overexpression from pCAMBIA3300-GFP constructs
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Function of GmVTC2b is antagonized by GmPLP1 protein interaction; transcript level under high light may show feedback related to AsA status.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GmVTC2b protein accumulates under high light. 2. GmVTC2b overexpression increases total ascorbic acid content, especially under high white light. 3. GmVTC2b overexpression improves PSII performance and lowers ROS under high light. 4. Its protective effect is strengthened when GmPLP1 is interfered with and weakened when GmPLP1 is overexpressed.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Overexpression or activation of GmVTC2b is a useful strategy to boost leaf ascorbate and high-light tolerance; can be stacked with GmPLP1 suppression for stronger effect.
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq context reported for GmPLP1 study only; NCBI SRA BioProject SUB11096210; SRR18063405; SRR18063406; SRR18063402; SRR18063398; SRR18063404; SRR18063403; SRR18063401; SRR18063400; SRR18063399

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: L-galactose pathway of ascorbic acid biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Converts GDP-L-galactose to L-galactose-1-phosphate in the ascorbic acid biosynthetic pathway.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: forms direct pathway intermediate leading to target product
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Supported as a key rate-limiting/control step in AsA biosynthesis and the enzyme activity assayed here is decisive for AsA accumulation under high light.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Overexpression increases AsA accumulation and high-light tolerance while lowering ROS accumulation.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Stable overexpression; total AsA assays; Fv/Fm imaging; DAB/NBT/H2O2 assays; transient expression; protein interaction with GmPLP1; inferred/associated GGP enzyme activity assays under different GmPLP1 backgrounds
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Protein induced by high white light and by high blue/high red light
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Protein induced by high white light and by high blue/high red light
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: high white light; high blue light; high red light
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper emphasizes GmVTC2b among three homologs (GmVTC2a/b/c) as the functionally characterized core enzyme in soybean high-light tolerance.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GmVTC2b is induced by high light. 2. GmVTC2b overexpression elevates leaf ascorbate. 3. GmVTC2b improves resistance to photodamage and ROS accumulation under high light. 4. GmPLP1 negatively modulates its activity through blue-light-sensitive interaction.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong target for engineering antioxidant capacity and high-light resilience in soybean, especially in combination with GmPLP1 suppression.
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: proGmVTC2b for tagged expression; transient pCAMBIA3300-GFP construct

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: L-galactose pathway of ascorbic acid biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: likely forms pathway intermediate to ascorbic acid
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Y2H; pull-down/BiFC support in supplementary interaction assays with GmPLP1
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Identified as a homolog interacting with GmPLP1, but not functionally characterized for metabolite/phenotype in the Results as a main experimental gene.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GmVTC2a is a GmVTC2 homolog. 2. It interacts with GmPLP1 in interaction assays. 3. No direct metabolic phenotyping was reported here.

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: L-galactose pathway of ascorbic acid biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: likely forms pathway intermediate to ascorbic acid
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Y2H; pull-down/BiFC support in supplementary interaction assays with GmPLP1
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Identified as a homolog interacting with GmPLP1, but not functionally characterized for metabolite/phenotype in the Results as a main experimental gene.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GmVTC2c is a GmVTC2 homolog. 2. It interacts with GmPLP1 in interaction assays. 3. No direct metabolic phenotyping was reported here.
